document.title: fmrilatent Transport-Backed Latent Representation PRD and Implementation Plan

executive_summary.problem_statement: fmrilatent currently supports explicit factorized latent representations and lightweight decoder-backed implicit representations, but it does not yet expose a first-class protocol for transport-backed latent analysis where a shared template basis is observed through subject-specific operators compiled outside the package.
executive_summary.proposed_solution: Extend fmrilatent so it can own the semantics of an analysis-ready latent field while delegating geometry compilation to neurofunctor and neurotransform. The initial implementation should represent transport-backed models as an ImplicitLatent family backed by a shared BasisTemplate asset, an external ObservationOperator, and explicit coefficient-space analysis coordinates.
executive_summary.architectural_thesis: Inter-subject anatomy belongs in the observation operator, not in the latent representation.
executive_summary.delivery_recommendation: Do not create a new top-level package. Deliver a thin vertical slice first: one shared template basis, one compiled observer, one operator-backed latent object, one coefficient recovery path, and one projection path usable by fmrireg.
decision_rationale: neurofunctor now provides a credible operator substrate with TransportOperator and ObservationOperator support.
fmrilatent already has the right latent/template primitives but needs a stronger latent protocol.
The change can be delivered incrementally without destabilizing existing LatentNeuroVec workflows.

strengths: LatentNeuroVec already provides explicit factorized latent storage.
ImplicitLatent already provides decoder-backed reconstruction for families where explicit matrices are not the right representation.
ParcelBasisTemplate and HierarchicalBasisTemplate already establish reusable template-asset patterns.
encode() and latent_factory() already provide a family-oriented entry point.
limitations: ImplicitLatent has no first-class coefficient-space protocol.
There is no generic contract for analysis coordinates, coefficient metrics, or covariance pushforward.
Template projection is currently explicit-loadings-centric and does not generalize to external observation operators.
There is no operator-aware encode path for solving Y ~= HZ.

neurofunctor.strategic_value: neurofunctor can now build and validate subject-specific transport and observation operators without owning basis estimation or GLM logic.

source_evidence.relevance: Package vision already prefers two object shapes and reusable template assets.
Shows the current minimal implicit latent contract.
Shows reusable shared template dictionaries and dual-solve projection.
Shows a second reusable template asset pattern.

product_definition.name: Transport-Backed Latent Representation Layer
product_definition.one_sentence_description: A latent representation layer for fMRI in which fmrilatent owns shared basis assets, coefficient recovery, and reconstruction semantics, while subject anatomy is carried by external observation operators.
target_users: method developers experimenting with shared template bases and subject-native inference
advanced users who need reproducible latent encoding across subjects
product_definition.core_user_story: As a developer, I want to encode native-space subject data against a shared template basis using a compiled observation operator so that I can fit models in coefficient space and render native or template maps only when needed.
primary_use_cases: Shared template spectral basis with subject-specific native-space coefficient recovery
Operator-backed reconstruction of native or template maps from latent coefficients
Downstream coefficient-space GLM fitting in fmrireg
Template-based reuse of fixed dictionaries across subjects without dense back-warped basis materialization

goals.goal: Make operator-backed latent representations a first-class concept in fmrilatent.
Preserve existing LatentNeuroVec and current ImplicitLatent workflows while adding a stronger latent protocol.
Allow fmrireg to consume coefficient-space responses transparently through a stable latent interface.
Keep package boundaries clean: geometry in neurotransform, operator compilation in neurofunctor, latent semantics in fmrilatent, GLM fitting in fmrireg.
Deliver a thin vertical slice before broadening basis-family coverage.

non_goals.non_goal: Do not move warp composition, interpolation kernels, or Jacobian math into fmrilatent.
Do not add GLM fitting, contrasts, AR models, or group inference to fmrilatent.
Do not create a new transport-specific top-level package in v1.
Do not require all basis families to support transport-backed operation in the first release.
Do not hard-wire half-density transport as the only amplitude policy in v1.

in_scope: A generalized BasisTemplate abstraction in fmrilatent
Cross-package contract definition for fmrireg integration
Tests and benchmarks for operator-backed latent recovery and reconstruction
out_of_scope: Generalized manifold metric estimation beyond optional placeholders
Automatic basis discovery pipelines inside neurofunctor
A full user-facing workflow wrapper spanning all packages in v1
assumptions: neurofunctor operator support remains stable enough to target.
fmrireg will consume a latent protocol rather than reaching into fmrilatent internals.
Shared basis estimation may come from fmrilatent builders or external tools such as genpca.
Matrix-free operator application is acceptable for v1 and may be preferable to dense basis materialization.

owns: shared basis estimation when generalized metrics or matrix-free decomposition are needed

object_model.name: BasisTemplate
object_model.purpose: Common contract for reusable shared template basis assets.

object_model.name: TransportBackedImplicitLatent
object_model.kind: ImplicitLatent family, not necessarily a new S4 class in v1
object_model.purpose: An analysis-ready latent object storing coefficients, basis asset reference, observation operator handle, and projection semantics.

object_model.name: ObservationOperator
object_model.purpose: Coefficient-space to sampled-domain operator with forward and adjoint access.

design_principles: Prefer protocol extension over ontology explosion.
Keep transport-backed support within the existing explicit vs implicit latent split.
Store enough metadata to make coefficient-space analysis reproducible.
Require explicit handling of coefficient metrics and projection covariance.
v1_decisions: Transport-backed latent objects should be implemented as an ImplicitLatent family first.
fmrilatent should consume operators through generic forward/adjoint contracts rather than hard-coding neurofunctor internals.
Half-density amplitude correction should be an operator option, not an invariant assumption.
Basis assets may be explicit or handle-backed.
deferred_decisions: Whether a dedicated S4 TransportedLatent class becomes necessary later.
Whether BasisTemplate should become S4 immediately or remain an S3/S4 mixed contract at first.
Whether residual covariance across coefficients should be modeled directly inside fmrireg v1 or deferred to v2.

latent_generics_to_add_in_fmrilatent.name: coef_time
latent_generics_to_add_in_fmrilatent.purpose: Return time by component coefficient matrices in either raw or analysis coordinates.

latent_generics_to_add_in_fmrilatent.name: coef_metric
latent_generics_to_add_in_fmrilatent.purpose: Return the coefficient-space metric or NULL/identity when analysis coordinates are Euclidean.

latent_generics_to_add_in_fmrilatent.name: analysis_transform
latent_generics_to_add_in_fmrilatent.purpose: Describe how raw coefficients are mapped into analysis coordinates.

latent_generics_to_add_in_fmrilatent.name: basis_asset
latent_generics_to_add_in_fmrilatent.purpose: Return the shared basis asset behind a latent object.

latent_generics_to_add_in_fmrilatent.name: decoder
latent_generics_to_add_in_fmrilatent.purpose: Return a callable decoder or operator-backed view for a requested output space.

latent_generics_to_add_in_fmrilatent.name: project_effect
latent_generics_to_add_in_fmrilatent.purpose: Project coefficient-space effect vectors to a requested image/sample space.

latent_generics_to_add_in_fmrilatent.name: project_vcov
latent_generics_to_add_in_fmrilatent.signature: project_vcov(x, Sigma, space = c("native", "template"), diag_only = TRUE, ...)
latent_generics_to_add_in_fmrilatent.purpose: Push coefficient-space covariance into the requested output space.

template_generics_to_add_or_generalize.name: template_project_operator
template_generics_to_add_or_generalize.purpose: Generalize template projection beyond explicit loadings so a basis asset can pair with an external observation operator.

template_generics_to_add_or_generalize.name: template_rank
template_generics_to_add_or_generalize.purpose: Provide a canonical basis rank query for templates.

template_generics_to_add_or_generalize.name: template_domain
template_generics_to_add_or_generalize.purpose: Return the domain/hash/space associated with the template asset.

encoding_entry_points.name: encode_operator
encoding_entry_points.purpose: Solve Y ~= HZ for a provided observation operator.

encoding_entry_points.name: encode_transport
encoding_entry_points.purpose: User-facing wrapper for transport-backed encoding using basis assets and compiled operators.

expected_external_operator_contract: provenance or metadata access where available

functional_requirements.requirement: fmrilatent must support a transport-backed latent object that stores coefficient payload, shared basis asset reference, and observation operator reference.
fmrilatent must support operator-aware coefficient recovery for Y ~= HZ with configurable regularization.
fmrilatent must expose analysis coordinates distinct from raw coefficient coordinates when the underlying basis geometry is non-Euclidean.
fmrilatent must reconstruct native-space and template-space views on demand without requiring permanent dense materialization of back-warped basis images.
fmrilatent must project coefficient-space effect vectors and covariance matrices into output spaces for downstream inference.
BasisTemplate implementations must provide enough metadata for reproducible replay across subjects.
Existing explicit latent workflows using LatentNeuroVec must continue to function unchanged.
Existing decoder-only ImplicitLatent workflows must remain compatible with reconstruct_matrix() and reconstruct_array().
The integration point with fmrireg must be package-level generics and data contracts, not direct knowledge of neurofunctor implementation details.
The first supported transport-backed workflow must include at least one reusable shared template basis family.

non_functional_requirements.requirement: Transport-backed encoding must support matrix-free operator application.
The new APIs must be deterministic for fixed basis assets, operators, and solver seeds.
Operator-backed latent workflows must carry provenance sufficient for debugging and reviewer inspection.
The extension should avoid unnecessary hard imports if a generic contract can be used instead.
The implementation must scale to moderate component counts without materializing dense voxel by component matrices unless explicitly requested.
The API should keep backward-compatible defaults for existing families.

success_metrics.target: One end-to-end transport-backed workflow passes tests from basis asset through latent encoding and projection.
A third-party reviewer can identify package boundaries and core contracts without ambiguity.
Existing LatentNeuroVec and current ImplicitLatent test suites remain green.
project_effect() and project_vcov() agree with materialized reference computations within numerical tolerance on toy problems.
At least one matrix-free observation operator path is used in tests rather than only explicit basis multiplication.

delivery_plan.overall_strategy: Deliver in phases, starting with protocol and one end-to-end vertical slice, then broaden basis support and downstream integration.

phases.name: Protocol Design and Contract Freeze
phases.objective: Define the minimal latent protocol fmrilatent will expose for transport-backed analysis.
tasks: Finalize the list of new generics.
Define BasisTemplate common contract.
Define the minimum external operator contract fmrilatent will rely on.
Document raw versus analysis coordinate semantics.
exit_criteria: Maintainer agrees no new top-level package is needed.
Maintainer agrees transport-backed support will begin as an ImplicitLatent family.
A stable minimal API list is approved.

phases.name: BasisTemplate Generalization
phases.objective: Generalize current template assets into a reusable basis-asset protocol.
tasks: Add BasisTemplate contract methods.
Adapt ParcelBasisTemplate to the shared contract.
Adapt HierarchicalBasisTemplate to the shared contract.
Add template rank/domain/provenance helpers.
deliverables: Updated tests for parcel and hierarchical templates
exit_criteria: At least two template asset types satisfy the same contract.

phases.name: Operator-Aware Latent Core
phases.objective: Introduce transport-backed latent semantics into fmrilatent without breaking existing families.
tasks: Add coef_time(), coef_metric(), analysis_transform(), basis_asset(), decoder(), project_effect(), and project_vcov().
Extend ImplicitLatent metadata and helper constructors to store operator-backed semantics.
Implement a transport-backed ImplicitLatent family constructor.
Define provenance fields for operator-backed latent objects.
exit_criteria: A transport-backed latent object can return coefficients in analysis coordinates.
project_effect() can emit native and template views on toy examples.

phases.name: Operator-Aware Encoding
phases.objective: Recover coefficients from observed data through external operators.
tasks: Implement encode_operator() / encode_transport().
Add ridge and optional temporal smoothing penalties.
Support explicit offsets and reproducible centering.
Add solver diagnostics and conditioning checks.
deliverables: Reference tests against explicit small-matrix solves
exit_criteria: Y ~= HZ recovery works on toy and integration fixtures.
Recovered coefficients reconstruct expected outputs within tolerance.

phases.name: fmrireg Vertical Slice
phases.objective: Prove that coefficient-space modeling can consume the new latent protocol cleanly.
tasks: Align with fmrireg latent dataset expectations.
Prototype a coefficient-space engine using the new protocol.
Validate effect and covariance projection into native/template views.
deliverables: One or more integration tests spanning packages
exit_criteria: A coefficient-space GLM can run without bespoke one-off adapters.
Beta and SE maps can be projected from coefficient space.

phases.name: Hardening and Expansion
phases.objective: Broaden basis-family coverage and improve performance, diagnostics, and documentation.
tasks: Add more basis-asset implementations as needed.
Benchmark matrix-free versus materialized paths.
Add vignette-level documentation.
Expand provenance, QC, and reviewer-facing documentation.
exit_criteria: At least one additional basis family beyond the initial slice is supported.
Documentation is sufficient for external contributors.

initial_basis_family_recommendation.recommended_choice: Start with an existing reusable shared basis asset already natural to fmrilatent, preferably hierarchical or parcel-based, before moving to a more ambitious template spectral basis estimated externally.
rationale: The asset and dual-solve patterns already exist.
The initial problem should test operator-backed latent semantics, not basis estimation novelty.
This keeps the first vertical slice small and reviewable.

initial_solver_strategy.approach: Use regularized least squares in coefficient space with optional temporal smoothing. Keep the first implementation deterministic and debuggable.

dependency_strategy.preferred: Depend on a generic operator contract rather than importing neurofunctor internals broadly.
dependency_strategy.acceptable_v1: A soft or suggested dependency if needed for examples and tests.
avoid: hard-coding knowledge of neurofunctor cache structure
hard-coding provenance list shape beyond stable public fields

focus: BasisTemplate plus ObservationOperator plus transport-backed latent object

required_test_fixtures: Toy explicit observation operator with known closed-form solution
Toy transport operator path from neurofunctor
A small reusable template basis fixture
A coefficient covariance fixture for projection tests
numerical_checks: projection covariance agreement against dense formulas
quality_gates: Focused integration tests spanning operator-backed latent paths

risks_and_mitigations.risk: The new operator layer in neurofunctor changes before fmrilatent integration stabilizes.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Target the smallest stable public contract only: forward, adjoint, n_source, n_target, and basic provenance.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: ImplicitLatent becomes overloaded and semantically inconsistent.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Define a strict transport-backed family contract and add protocol generics rather than encoding everything in ad hoc metadata.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: fmrireg integration leaks package responsibilities back into fmrilatent.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Keep modeling logic in fmrireg and limit fmrilatent responsibilities to representation, encoding, and projection.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Coefficient metrics are ignored or inconsistently handled.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Make analysis coordinates and coefficient metrics explicit API concepts in v1.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Matrix-free implementations are hard to debug.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Keep small explicit reference fixtures and require dense-agreement tests on toy examples.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: A new abstraction layer creates unnecessary API complexity.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Prefer extension of existing classes and generics over introducing many new public classes.

alternatives_considered.alternative: Create a new top-level transport framework package
alternatives_considered.reason: Too much ontology too early; the existing ecosystem already has a natural decomposition.

alternatives_considered.alternative: Make transport-backed support a new concrete class hierarchy immediately
alternatives_considered.reason: Transport-backed support can begin as an ImplicitLatent family while the protocol stabilizes.

alternatives_considered.alternative: Materialize back-warped basis images and treat them as ordinary explicit loadings
alternatives_considered.reason: This hides operator geometry, makes coefficient-space semantics harder to reason about, and can force unnecessary dense materialization.

open_questions.question: Should BasisTemplate become a formal S4 class now, or remain a shared method contract across existing template asset types in v1?
Should fmrilatent add neurofunctor as an Imports dependency, a Suggests dependency, or rely on generic interoperability only?
Which basis asset should anchor the first end-to-end slice: hierarchical, parcel, or an externally estimated spectral basis?
Should analysis coordinates always be Euclideanized in fmrilatent, or should fmrireg be allowed to consume explicit coefficient metrics?
How much operator provenance must be preserved in latent objects versus queried lazily from external operator instances?

acceptance_criteria: A third-party reviewer agrees the package boundaries are coherent and non-overlapping.
fmrilatent exposes a documented latent protocol for operator-backed coefficient-space analysis.
At least one reusable basis asset can be paired with an external observation operator to recover coefficients and reconstruct views.
project_effect() and project_vcov() work on operator-backed latent objects and are numerically validated.
Existing explicit and current implicit latent workflows remain green.
A documented path into fmrireg exists without pushing GLM logic into fmrilatent.

review_checklist.question: Are package responsibilities separated cleanly across neurotransform, neurofunctor, fmrilatent, and fmrireg?
Is the choice to use an ImplicitLatent family first defensible, or should a dedicated class be introduced immediately?
Are the proposed fmrilatent generics sufficient for downstream modeling and reconstruction?
Does the delivery plan prove value early enough with a thin vertical slice?
Are there hidden coupling risks in the dependency strategy?
Is there any missing acceptance criterion required before implementation should start?

recommended_next_action.action: Review and approve Phase P0 plus the required fmrilatent protocol surface before implementation begins.
recommended_next_action.why: The critical risk is not missing geometry support; it is letting transport-backed latent semantics remain implicit and ad hoc.
